book 1 /bʊk/ ●●● S1 W1 noun     1 printed pages [countable] a set of printed pages held together in a cover so that you can read them I've just started reading a book by Graham Greene. a cookery book book about/on a book about cats 2 part of a book [countable] one of the parts that a very large book is divided into book of the Book of Isaiah 3 →books 4 grammar patterns example [countable] a record kept in two parts book a table (=in a restaurant) I'll book a table for 7.30 tomorrow evening. 5 subsense example a first meaning of the subsense subsense example one b second meaning of the subsense subsense example two From Longman Business Dictionary book 1 /bʊk/ noun 1 books [plural] ACCOUNTING the accounting records of a business SYN ACCOUNT BOOKS The company's books are in such chaos that we won't know the truth for some time.
